subject: Physics
title: The Nuclear Waste Problem
description: One-minute skill: The Nuclear Waste Problem.

hook.text: Spent nuclear fuel remains dangerously radioactive for tens of thousands of years. Where do you put it?

buildup.text: Most waste sits in temporary pools at reactor sites. They were never meant for permanent storage.

discovery.text: Deep geological repositories—burying waste in stable rock formations—are the leading long-term solution.

twist.text: Finland opened the world's first permanent repository in 2023. Most countries haven't even started.

climax.text: The challenge is marking sites so humans 10,000 years from now know not to dig. Languages may not survive.

punchline.text: Waste that outlasts civilizations. Nuclear power's hardest problem.

quiz.question: What is the leading solution for permanent nuclear waste storage?
options: Burying it deep in stable geological formations
